The '''''Avatar Orbital Platform''''' was a joint venture between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and a group of [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] [[Slavery/Legends|slavers]] called the [[Blackscale Clan/Legends|Blackscale Clan]], built to aid in the transportation and sale of [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] slaves captured on nearby [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] and the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Alaris Prime/Legends|Alaris Prime]].

==Description==
''Avatar Orbital Platform'' was a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] which acted as a [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] hub. It was constructed by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] slave groups. Not built for looks, the platform was crude but functional, and was equipped with sufficient defenses to keep [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] from interfering with the Imperial slave trade. As of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], it was configured in a square, with the main [[Reactor/Legends|reactor]] in the middle. Four [[Airbase/Legends|airbase]]s provided [[Transport/Legends|transports]] on and off the station. Military buildings protected the reactor. At one corner was an Imperial [[Fortress/Legends|fortress]]; the corner diagonal to it held a slave pen protected by four [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser turrets]]. A complement of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] was also aboard.<ref name="SW:GB" />

==History==
''Avatar'' was frequently the last stop for an enslaved family before they were separated for shipment to their new masters. The slaver [[Pekt]] was instrumental in designing the station.<ref name="SW:GB" /> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], only the Blackscale Clan provided clearance codes to land on the platform. However both the [[Zssik Clan/Legends|Zssik Clan]] of Trandoshan slavers and Imperial scientists on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Kashyyyk managed to acquire the codes to send operatives aboard the station.<ref name="Rage" />

[[File:Blackdcale_Enforcer.jpg|thumb|left|A Blackscale Clan Enforcer]]
In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], Wookiee freedom fighters attacked the ''Avatar Platform'' to stop the Trandoshan slaving operations. During these fights, a group of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] working for the Zssik Clan entered the station to disrupt the Blackscale's slaving operations. After battling the Blackscale forces aboard the station, the spacers activated the station's self-destruct sequence and returned to Kashyyyk. Large explosions extensively damaged the station and killed most beings on board, but overall the station remained intact and quite functional. Before long, both the Blackscale Clan and Wookiee freedom fighters sent reinforcements to the station to continue the fight for control over the ''Avatar Platform''.<ref name="Rage" />

During the [[Liberation of Kashyyyk/Legends|Liberation of Kashyyyk]] in 4 ABY, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] pursued [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Hindane Darcc/Legends|Hindane Darcc]] and Pekt. When [[Darcc's Palace|their palace]] was destroyed, Darcc and Pekt retreated to ''Avatar'' but were followed by their pursuers. Solo had already sent a message to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and as soon as they arrived on ''Avatar'', they were joined by [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] on the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] and [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] with [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. After a brief battle, the Rebels destroyed the airbases to prevent Darcc and Pekt from escaping, rescued [[Attichitcuk/Legends|Attichitcuk]], who was enslaved with other Wookiees, and managed to kill Darcc and Pekt. Finally, the Rebels destroyed the main reactor, destabilizing the ''Avatar'' and causing it to crash onto Kashyyyk.<ref name="SW:GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Avatar Orbital Platform'' was first mentioned in the [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] for the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''. The station appeared in the game's Chewbacca campaign. The station appeared again in [[2005]]'s ''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Rage of the Wookiees]]'', the second expansion of ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]''. In both games, the station is destroyed by the player's actions. However after the station's destruction in ''Rage of the Wookiees''—which takes place several years before the events of the Chewbacca campaign in ''Galactic Battlegrounds''—the non-player character [[Marium Valmont]] explains that the station wasn't fully destroyed and remained "quite functional."

In ''Galactic Battlegrounds'', ''Avatar'' is an open, flat series of wide-walled platforms. This is due to the limitations of the game, which does not support fighting in covered buildings or starships. The player has 10 minutes in the beginning of the mission to destroy the airbases, and is then tasked with rescuing the Wookiee prisoners, destroying the fortress, and killing Darcc and Pekt. After destroying the reactor, the Wookiee units escape in a scripted event.
